'''Sean Rutgerson''' (born 9 February 1978) is the head coach of the [[United States national rugby league team|United States]] and a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. His primary position was as {{rlp|pr}}, but he also played in the {{rlp|sr}}. Over his career, Rutgerson played for the [[North Sydney Bears]] and the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the Australian [[National Rugby League]], the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[Jacksonville Axemen]] in the [[AMNRL]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.americanrugbynews.com/artman/publish/rugby_league/Game_of_the_Week_Top_of_the_table_clash.shtml|title=Game of the Week: Top of the table clash|publisher=American Rugby News|date=2008-06-18|accessdate=2008-06-19}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Rutgerson started his professional career in Australia with the North Sydney, where he played. After two years at North Sydney, he moved to the [[Canberra Raiders]] where he spent four years and scored his first try at the top level.

He signed for Salford City Reds in 2004 and played three seasons in Super League. He then moved to the US, where he played for the [[Jacksonville Axemen]] and also took on a coaching role at the club.

In the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]] Rutgerson represented [[South Africa national rugby league team|South Africa]]. In all three games for South Africa, Rutgerson started in the {{rlp|sr}}, rather than his typical position as {{rlp|pr}}.

==Coaching==
Rutgerson was appointed assistant coach of the USA national team under [[Brian McDermott (rugby league)|Brian McDermott]] in 2015,<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20150714181635/http://asia.eurosport.com/rugby-league/mcdermott-lands-usa-head-coach-role_sto4812178/story.shtml McDermott lands USA head coach role]</ref> eventually taking over from McDermott as head coach in 2018.
